# OS Config Receiver

<!-- status autogenerated section -->
| Status | |

[development]: https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-collector/blob/main/docs/component-stability.md#development

<!-- end autogenerated section -->

## Overview

The `osconfig` receiver collects Linux OS and kernel configuration variables and emits them as OpenTelemetry log records. This data is consumed by the DBO Advisors feature for database advisory checks.

**Supported platform:** Linux only. On non-Linux platforms the receiver compiles but logs a warning and produces no data.

## Collected Variables

| # | Variable | Source Path |
|---|----------|-------------|
| 1 | `vm.swappiness` | `/proc/sys/vm/swappiness` |
| 2 | `sys.kernel.mm.transparent_hugepage.enabled` | `/sys/kernel/mm/transparent_hugepage/enabled` |
| 3 | `net.ipv4.tcp_keepalive_time` | `/proc/sys/net/ipv4/tcp_keepalive_time` |
| 4 | `fs.file_max` | `/proc/sys/fs/file-max` |
| 5 | `kernel.pid_max` | `/proc/sys/kernel/pid_max` |
| 6 | `kernel.threads_max` | `/proc/sys/kernel/threads-max` |
| 7 | `swap_total` | `/proc/meminfo` (SwapTotal field) |
| 8 | Mount options (per mount point): | |
|   | - `queue/scheduler` | `/sys/block/<dev>/queue/scheduler` |
|   | - `queue/read_ahead_kb` | `/sys/block/<dev>/queue/read_ahead_kb` |
|   | - `fs_type` | `/proc/mounts` |
|   | - `config` | Mount option flags (e.g., `rw,noatime,errors=remount-ro`) |

## Configuration

```yaml
receivers:
  osconfig:
    # How often to collect configuration variables. Default: 5m
    collection_interval: 1h
    # Individual variables can be enabled/disabled.
    variables:
      vm.swappiness:
        enabled: true
      sys.kernel.mm.transparent_hugepage.enabled:
        enabled: true
      net.ipv4.tcp_keepalive_time:
        enabled: true
      fs.file_max:
        enabled: true
      kernel.pid_max:
        enabled: true
      kernel.threads_max:
        enabled: true
      swap_total:
        enabled: true
      mount_options:
        enabled: true

service:
  pipelines:
    logs:
      receivers: [osconfig]
      exporters: [debug]
```

### Configuration Options

| Option | Type | Default | Description |
|--------|------|---------|-------------|
| `collection_interval` | duration | `1h` | How often to collect host configuration variables |
| `variables` | map | all enabled | Map of variable names to their enable/disable config |

## Output Format

Each collection cycle produces a single OTel log record containing all configuration variables:

- **Scope name**: `github.com/solarwinds-cloud/solarwinds-otel-collector-contrib/receiver/osconfigreceiver`
- **Severity**: Info (9)
- **Timestamp**: Collection time
- **Attributes**:
  - `config` (string) — JSON object containing all configuration variables

### Config Attribute Format

The `config` attribute is a JSON string with a flat key-value structure:

- **System variables**: Use the variable name as-is
  - Example: `"vm.swappiness": "60"`

- **Mount-related variables**: Use the format `mount|<path>|<name>`
  - Example: `"mount|/|fs_type": "ext4"`
  - Example: `"mount|/boot/efi|queue/scheduler": "[mq-deadline] none"`

#### Example Output

```json
{
  "vm.swappiness": "60",
  "net.ipv4.tcp_keepalive_time": "7200",
  "sys.kernel.mm.transparent_hugepage.enabled": "[always] madvise never",
  "fs.file_max": "9223372036854775807",
  "kernel.pid_max": "4194304",
  "kernel.threads_max": "506403",
  "swap_total": "8388604 kB",
  "mount|/|fs_type": "ext4",
  "mount|/|config": "rw,relatime,errors=remount-ro",
  "mount|/|queue/scheduler": "[mq-deadline] none",
  "mount|/|queue/read_ahead_kb": "128",
  "mount|/boot/efi|fs_type": "vfat",
  "mount|/boot/efi|config": "rw,relatime,fmask=0077,dmask=0077",
  "mount|/boot/efi|queue/scheduler": "[mq-deadline] none",
  "mount|/boot/efi|queue/read_ahead_kb": "128"
}
```

## Privileges

No elevated privileges are required. All source files are world-readable on standard Linux systems.
